targets.name: tradingview-es-futures
targets.goal: S&P 500 E-mini front-month futures (ES) primary mini-chart on the symbol overview page — TradingView's tv-lightweight-charts canvas card.
landmarks: Top of the symbol overview page, the primary chart card (NOT the related-symbols mini-charts further down)
Rendered with TradingView's lightweight-charts JS lib — outer container has class 'tv-lightweight-charts' and a <table> with two <canvas> overlays
There are multiple mini-charts on the page; the page's primary one is indexed 1
stable_attrs: data-lightweight-mini-chart-id="1" — primary chart (id="2", "3"... are related/secondary charts)
class 'tv-lightweight-charts' marks the chart wrapper but matches all charts on the page; combine with data-lightweight-mini-chart-id for uniqueness
Class 'chart-XXXXXX' has a hash suffix that changes between deploys — DO NOT rely on it

targets.name: yahoo-finance-markets
targets.goal: Yahoo Finance /markets/ page — the 'Americas' regional indices table card (S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq, Russell 2000, etc.).
landmarks: URL is /markets/ (NOT the homepage / which now redirects/restructures)
Page lists four regional sections: 'World Indices' (summary), 'Americas', 'Europe', 'Asia' — each is a <section data-testid='world-indices'> sibling
We want the one whose direct-child header h3 is 'Americas'
stable_attrs: data-testid="world-indices" — applies to all four sections, so SCOPE BY HEADER TEXT
Direct-child path 'section > header > h3' containing the region name is reliable
Class names like 'yf-1tpeyy7', 'yf-8abkgo' have build-hash suffixes — DO NOT use
data-testid-header="ticker" / 'sparkline' / 'price' on the table headers are also stable

targets.name: yahoo-finance-markets-id
targets.goal: Yahoo Finance /markets/ page — the S&P 500 sector heatmap (Treemap). Each tile is a sector colored by day return; tooltip shows day/YTD return + market weight.
landmarks: Below the indices tables on the /markets/ page
Visual treemap with rectangular tiles per sector (Technology, Financials, etc.)
Each tile is an <a class='rect-container'> with id=sector-slug and data-index
stable_attrs: section[data-testid="heatmap"] — exact, stable
Inner div has role='region' aria-label='Heatmap'
Tile <a> elements have stable id (sector slug, e.g., 'technology', 'financials') and href='/sectors/<slug>/'
Class names like 'yf-1whrcng', 'yf-1aqyc8a' are build-hash suffixed — DO NOT use

targets.name: cboe-vix
targets.goal: VIX product page chart panel — capture the default 1M chart first, then click the 'Intraday' radio button and capture again. Page is built with Radix UI + Recharts + Tailwind.
landmarks: Chart container is a <div> with attribute data-chart starting with 'chart-' and data-variant='light' (the value after 'chart-' is dynamic per render)
Timeframe row is a Radix UI RadioGroup: <button role='radio'> with text 'Intraday', '1M', '3M', '1Y'. Active button has data-state='on' / aria-checked='true'.
Chart and buttons are siblings inside the same panel under main content section
stable_attrs: data-chart^='chart-' (prefix is stable; suffix is a Radix-generated id like «...»)
data-variant='light' (theme variant)
button[role='radio'] with normalize-space()='Intraday' (or '1M'/'3M'/'1Y') — text + role is the most stable handle
Avoid Radix-generated radix-«...» ids — they regenerate on each render

targets.name: vixcentral-term
targets.goal: VIX futures term-structure chart on vixcentral.com — Highcharts SVG drawn into a hardcoded #container1 div sized 800x400.
landmarks: First chart on the page, top-left
Hardcoded outer wrapper: <div id='container1' class='ui-widget' style='width: 800px; height: 400px'>
Highcharts JS injects an <svg> with a <rect class='highcharts-background'> filling 800x400; chart needs ~5-8s after page load to draw
stable_attrs: id='container1' is hardcoded by the site author and stable
Inside the SVG, 'rect.highcharts-background' is the chart frame; 'g.highcharts-axis' marks axes
delay_wait must be >=5s so Highcharts has time to render before screenshot — capturing too early yields a blank 800x400 box
Site uses bot-detection that blocks rendering with default headless UA — capture must use a realistic UA and --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled (already wired into cap_web._LAUNCH_ARGS)

targets.name: tradingview-ng-futures
targets.goal: Natural Gas (NG) front-month futures primary mini-chart — same TradingView page template as ES futures.
landmarks: Same as tradingview-es-futures
stable_attrs: Same as tradingview-es-futures: data-lightweight-mini-chart-id="1" is the primary chart

targets.name: hull-sentiment-meter
targets.goal: Hull Tactical 'Market Sentiment Meter' panel — heading 'Market Sentiment Meter' with the current numeric reading (e.g., 'Current: 92'), a horizontal scale, and the gauge SVG image below it.
landmarks: Single panel labeled 'Market Sentiment Meter' with a 'Current: NN' span (id='meter_data')
Below the heading: a thin horizontal SVG bar (id='progress-bar') showing the position on the 0-100 scale, then a meter image '/_nuxt/img/<hash>.svg'
Page built with Nuxt; Vue scoped attribute is data-v-2d594030 on every element
stable_attrs: Outer wrapper class='progress-bar' is unique on the page (count=1)
Inner anchors: id='meter_data' (current value text), id='progress-bar' (inner SVG bar)
Avoid the deep //*[@id='__layout']/.../div[N]/... positional path — re-orderings of siblings break it

targets.name: finviz-sec-map
targets.goal: Finviz S&P 500 sector treemap — main canvas drawn inside the wrapper, excluding the transparent hover-overlay canvas.
landmarks: Two stacked <canvas> elements inside #canvas-wrapper: the main 'chart' canvas with the rendered map, and the 'hover-canvas' overlay used for tooltips
Main canvas has fallback text 'S&P 500 Map' and the class 'chart' (and 'initialized' once JS finishes drawing)
stable_attrs: id='canvas-wrapper' is stable
canvas.chart is the main map; canvas.hover-canvas is the overlay (avoid)
Class 'initialized' appears AFTER drawing — useful as a readiness gate but not strictly required for selection

targets.name: te-us-stock-market
targets.goal: TradingEconomics page for US stock market: (1) the textual description block summarizing recent index moves, and (2) the Highcharts chart inside the UpdatePanel (without surrounding panel chrome).
landmarks: Description: a prose block with id='description' under the page title
Chart: ASP.NET UpdatePanel id='UpdatePanelChart' wraps a Highcharts SVG with class 'highcharts-container'
stable_attrs: id='description' is stable (text block)
id='UpdatePanelChart' is stable (panel wrapper)

'#UpdatePanelChart .highcharts-container' isolates the chart SVG from the panel chrome (avoid plain '#UpdatePanelChart' which adds padding, and '#UpdatePanelChart svg' which also matches toolbar icon SVGs)
Site uses Highcharts; chart needs ~5-8s after load to draw. Bot-detection is non-trivial — capture must use a realistic UA (already wired into cap_web._LAUNCH_ARGS / element_checker)

targets.name: cnn-fear-greed
targets.goal: CNN Fear & Greed Index — the LARGE gauge dial in the main article body (NOT the small mini-gauge in the page-top ribbon).
landmarks: Page renders the gauge twice: a small mini variant in the top market ribbon, and the full-size gauge in the article body inside a tabbed container
We want the large one — has parent class 'market-fng-gauge' (without the '_mini' modifier)
SVG inside has id='fear-and-greed-dial' and viewBox='0 0 338 173'
stable_attrs: BEM classes 'market-fng-gauge', 'market-fng-gauge_mini' (mini variant), 'market-fng-gauge__meter-container', 'market-fng-gauge__meter', 'market-fng-gauge__dial' — all stable
data-index-label='greed'/'fear'/'neutral'/'extreme-fear'/'extreme-greed' on .market-fng-gauge__meter (state attribute)
id='fear-and-greed-dial' on the SVG (unique once the main gauge is rendered, but stale if mini renders first — prefer scoping via :not(_mini))

targets.name: te-natural-gas
targets.goal: TradingEconomics page for Henry Hub Natural Gas commodity: (1) prose description block summarizing recent price moves, (2) the Highcharts chart inside the UpdatePanel (without surrounding panel chrome).
landmarks: Description: a prose block with id='description' under the page title
Chart: ASP.NET UpdatePanel id='UpdatePanelChart' wraps a Highcharts SVG with class 'highcharts-container'
stable_attrs: id='description' is stable (a paragraph block, NOT the <meta name='description'> head tag)
id='UpdatePanelChart' is stable (panel wrapper)
'#UpdatePanelChart .highcharts-container' isolates the chart SVG from the panel chrome
Avoid plain '#UpdatePanelChart' (adds padding) and '#UpdatePanelChart svg' (also matches toolbar icon SVGs)
Site uses Highcharts; chart needs ~5-8s after load to draw. Bot-detection blocks plain headless — must use realistic UA

targets.name: te-eu-natural-gas
targets.goal: TradingEconomics page for European TTF natural gas commodity: ONLY the Highcharts chart (no description selector for this page).
landmarks: Chart: ASP.NET UpdatePanel id='UpdatePanelChart' wraps a Highcharts SVG with class 'highcharts-container'
stable_attrs: id='UpdatePanelChart' is stable (panel wrapper)
'#UpdatePanelChart .highcharts-container' isolates the chart SVG from the panel chrome
Avoid ASP.NET-generated ids like '#ctl00_ContentPlaceHolder1_*' — they hallucinate; the page actually uses the simple 'UpdatePanelChart' alias
Site uses Highcharts; chart needs ~5-8s after load to draw. Bot-detection blocks plain headless — must use realistic UA

targets.name: natgasweather-temp
targets.goal: Daily US temperature anomaly map image embedded in the natgasweather homepage post.
landmarks: WordPress post body (id like 'post-####')
An with src containing today's temperature/forecast image
stable_attrs: Post id is volatile (changes when post is reposted); prefer matching the section heading text 'Temperature' / 'GFS' / '7-Day' and selecting the nearest
img alt or src containing 'temp' / 'temperature' / 'gfs'

targets.name: eia-grid-generation
targets.goal: First panel of the EIA grid monitor 'Daily Generation Mix' dashboard — a stacked area chart of US48 generation by source.
landmarks: Angular SPA — components have tag names like 'app-grid', 'gridster-item', 'app-visualization'
First gridster-item is the stacked-area chart in the top-left tile
stable_attrs: Custom Angular tags 'app-grid', 'gridster-item' are stable
Prefer 'gridster-item:first-of-type app-visualization' over deep positional path

targets.name: coingecko-top
targets.goal: Bitcoin detail page price card on CoinGecko — name ('Bitcoin'), symbol label ('BTC Price'), market-cap rank, current USD price, and 24h percent change. Replaced the older homepage-table approach which became Cloudflare-flaky.
landmarks: Block on the right side of the coin detail page header showing the live price
Page is built with Stimulus.js controllers — elements use data-controller / data-*-target attributes
The same selector works on any /en/coins/<slug> page (substitute slug for ETH, etc.)
stable_attrs: data-coin-show-target="staticCoinPrice" — Stimulus target attribute, very stable (renaming would break the JS controller)
Inner: <h1> with the coin name and a sibling span 'BTC Price' / 'ETH Price'
Tailwind class names with hash-like prefixes (tw-flex, tw-gap-x-2) are stable but generic — do NOT rely on them alone
Class names like '!tw-text-base 2lg:!tw-text-lg' have build-version suffixes baked in — avoid
